ddls:
CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "country_code" TEXT /* Three-letter country code (e.g., USA, CAN) */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Total population of the country */,
  "continent_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the continent where the country is located */,
  "capital_city" TEXT /* Capital city of the country */,
  "area_km" REAL /* Area of the country in square kilometers */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_countries_continent_id FOREIGN KEY ("continent_id") REFERENCES continents ("continent_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "continents" (
  "continent_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each continent */,
  "continent_name" TEXT /* Name of the continent */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("continent_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "population_statistics" (
  "stat_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each statistical entry */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country associated with the statistics */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year for the statistical data */,
  "total_population" INTEGER /* Total population recorded for that year */,
  "male_population" INTEGER /* Number of males in the population */,
  "female_population" INTEGER /* Number of females in the population */,
  "urban_population" INTEGER /* Number of people living in urban areas */,
  "rural_population" INTEGER /* Number of people living in rural areas */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stat_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_population_statistics_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, administrator) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country data being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date of access */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "demographic_trends" (
  "trend_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each trend entry */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country associated with the trend */,
  "trend_description" TEXT /* Description of the trend (e.g., population growth, decline) */,
  "start_year" INTEGER /* Start year of the trend observation */,
  "end_year" INTEGER /* End year of the trend observation */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("trend_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographic_trends_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source (e.g., UN, World Bank) */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of source (e.g., governmental, non-governmental) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the source */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the data source */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_updates" (
  "update_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each update entry */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country whose data was updated */,
  "update_date" TEXT /* Date of the update */,
  "update_description" TEXT /* Description of the update made */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("update_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_updates_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "demographic_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "report_title" TEXT /* Title of the report */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date the report was generated */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "report_data" TEXT /* Data included in the report */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographic_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_source_reliability" (
  "reliability_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each reliability entry */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data source being evaluated */,
  "reliability_score" REAL /* Score indicating the reliability of the data source */,
  "reliability_description" TEXT /* Description of the reliability evaluation */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reliability_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_source_reliability_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id")
)
